title: Rumohra dimorphophylla
taxonomy: Rumohra dimorphophylla is a species of fern in the family Blechnaceae. It is native to South America, specifically in the subtropical and temperate regions. This species of fern has been classified under the genus Rumohra and is closely related to other ferns in the same family. Rumohra dimorphophylla is further classified under the kingdom Plantae and the phylum Pteridophyta, which includes all ferns. The classification of Rumohra dimorphophylla is also reflected in the International Plant Names Index and other botanical databases.
uses: Rumohra dimorphophylla, also known as the autumn fern or lady fern, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help in reducing swelling and pain in the body. In some cultures, it is used to treat various health issues, including fever, diarrhea, and respiratory problems. Additionally, the plant is believed to have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the body against free radicals and oxidative stress. However, more research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses of Rumohra dimorphophylla.

constituents: Rumohra dimorphophylla contains a variety of active constituents, including triterpenoid saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds.
